description_long: This object is a blocky, stylized airplane with a geometric, voxel-like design. The fuselage is thick and angular, with a prominent vertical stabilizer at the rear and a large, octagonal nose section. The wings are straight and extend horizontally from the midsection, with boxy engine nacelles or support structures attached beneath each wing. The tail section features a tall, upright fin and a horizontal stabilizer, giving the rear a distinctive T-tail appearance. The surface is uniformly gray, with no visible windows, doors, or detailed markings, and the overall form is highly simplified, suggesting a digital or toy-like representation.
description: A blocky, gray, voxel-style airplane with a thick fuselage, straight wings, and a prominent T-tail. The design is highly geometric and simplified, with no visible windows or markings.
description_view_0: The airplane is seen from the front, highlighting the octagonal nose, central fuselage, and the vertical stabilizer at the rear.
description_view_1: A top-down view reveals the straight wings extending from the midsection and the boxy structures beneath them, as well as the T-tail configuration.
description_view_2: The rear view emphasizes the vertical stabilizer and the symmetry of the tail section, with the wings and fuselage visible in profile.
description_view_3: An angled top-rear view shows the relationship between the wings, tail, and fuselage, with the blocky engine nacelles or supports clearly visible.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with straight, rectangular wings and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin blocky nacelles under each wing, engine type not visually specified.
attribute_tail_design: T-tail configuration with a single vertical stabilizer and a high-mounted horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted cockpit, enclosed, with no visible windows or canopy details.
